items.title: Shrink Org Logo for a Sleeker Look in .github 🎨
items.summary: This update reduces the width of the organization profile logo from 300px to 220px, ensuring a more compact appearance while maintaining high resolution. The change enhances the overall aesthetics of the README without altering any functionality.

items.title: Enhance MCP Servers Table with URL Column in .github 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new URL column to the MCP Servers table in the organization profile README. Each server's hosted Streamable-HTTP endpoint is now displayed alongside its GitHub repo and PyPI badge, improving accessibility and usability. The changes ensure that all 21 servers have valid URLs, with a scheduled workflow to maintain synchronization in future updates.

items.title: Optimize org logo size in .github 🎨
items.summary: The logo on the GitHub organization profile has been resized for better display. By switching from a Markdown image to an HTML `` tag with a specified width, the logo remains high-resolution while appearing smaller and more visually appealing.

items.title: Fix chat targeting to ensure messages go to the right recipient in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where WeChat messages could be sent to the wrong recipient due to stale chat windows. Key changes include implementing strict title matching for opened chats and enhancing the search functionality to retry and clear previous searches. The behavior now requires exact matches, preventing mis-sends and improving overall reliability.

items.title: Reconnect Stale DB Connections for Smooth Sync in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new function, `ensure_db_connection()`, to handle stale database connections during long-running sync operations. By pinging the database before processing each file, we prevent silent errors that could lead to dropped records. This change enhances reliability without altering behavior on healthy connections.

items.title: Added July 2026 Creator-Fee Buyback in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update incorporates the on-chain creator-fee buyback executed on July 1, 2026, into the buyback history. The transaction involved 37.69 USDC, resulting in the purchase of 78,441 ACE through the DFlow Aggregator v4. All data has been verified for accuracy and is now available for transparency.

items.title: Update Model Allowlists with New OpenAPI Docs in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the CLI allowlists for AiChat, GLM, and OpenAI models with the latest OpenAPI documentation. New models such as `gpt-5.4-mini`, `gpt-5.4-nano`, `glm-5`, and `glm-5-turbo` have been added, enhancing command option validation and model listings. The changes ensure that users can access the most current model options seamlessly.

items.title: Exciting New Models Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several new chat models to the public /models directory, including Claude Sonnet 5, GPT-5.4-mini, GPT-5.4-nano, GLM-5, and DeepSeek-V4-Flash. The models have been benchmarked and integrated with existing billing rules, ensuring a seamless experience for users. All changes have been verified with 856 passing tests, confirming the integrity of the updates.

items.title: Updated Featured Models for Current Flagships in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The Featured badge on the models directory has been refreshed to showcase the latest flagship chat models. Key updates include the addition of GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Grok 4, ensuring that only one current flagship per major provider is highlighted. This change enhances the user experience by providing up-to-date recommendations.

items.title: Sync model names to match OpenAPI spec in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the CLI with the latest AceDataCloud OpenAPI spec by removing the `-preview` suffix from two chat model identifiers. Key changes include updates to the `GEMINI_CHAT_MODELS` list, the display table in `output.py`, and the corresponding tests in `test_commands.py`. The README has also been revised to reflect these changes, ensuring consistency across documentation and functionality.

items.title: Fix task history retrieval issue in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where the `retrieve_batch` function returned a count greater than zero but an empty items array for many services. The root cause was identified as undefined keys being passed to MongoDB queries, which led to incorrect filtering. The fix involves stripping undefined keys before executing the query, ensuring accurate task history retrieval across ten affected workers.

items.title: Enhance Group Management Features in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the robustness of the WeChat group creation and invitation APIs, addressing issues found during live testing. Key changes include enhanced member selection verification, support for 微信号 input, and a new confirmation dialog for large group invitations. These enhancements ensure a smoother user experience and prevent silent errors during group management.

items.title: Transform Gemini Image Model for Compatibility in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a transformation for the official Gemini image model name, changing `gemini-3-pro-image` to `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`. This change ensures compatibility with upstream services and prevents mismatches in account matching and URL usage. The implementation has been verified with successful `py_compile` and `ruff` checks.

items.title: Expose Official Image Model IDs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the exposed image model IDs with Google's official Gemini API by removing the '-preview' suffix for external use. Key changes include modifications to the `_chat_models.json` for billing rules and updates to the `visible_models` interface to only show official names. Compatibility with the previous '-preview' naming is maintained internally, ensuring seamless transitions for existing users.

items.title: Update favicon for UnifiedHub center node in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The center node of the UnifiedHub has been updated to use the compact single-A favicon instead of the full wordmark logo. This change ensures consistency with the surrounding provider icons while maintaining a fallback to the packaged favicon for tenants without a configured one. The update is a simple one-line modification with no impact on logic or billing.

items.title: Align ephone 2.0 Default Resolution to Official Standard in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update corrects the default resolution for the ephone Seedance 2.0 model to 720p, aligning it with the official Volcengine ARK API specifications. Previously, the system defaulted to 1080p for certain models, which has now been fixed to ensure consistent billing and rendering. The change maintains support for explicit resolution requests while ensuring compliance with the official guidelines.

items.title: Exciting Updates: New Chat Models and Options in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces five new chat models and two new request parameters to the CLI, enhancing functionality and user experience. The new models include 'gemini-2.5-flash-lite' and 'gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview', among others. Additionally, the CLI now supports updated options for maximum completion tokens and service tier selection, streamlining command usage.

items.title: Enhance Gemini Image Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new branch in the image processing logic to ensure that `gemini-2.5-flash-image` is correctly passed through without being downgraded to a text model. The change prevents issues with account matching that could lead to incorrect outputs. All relevant tests have passed successfully.

items.title: Enhance Gemini Image Models and Fix Pricing Drift in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new image models, `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, with respective pricing of $0.14 and $0.35 per image. Additionally, it addresses a pricing drift issue by ensuring accurate cost calculations and updating the service display prices. The changes have been thoroughly tested and are ready for deployment.

items.title: Enhance MCP Tools with Backend Filters in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates previously unsupported backend filters into the AceDataCloud MCP tools, allowing for more precise query capabilities. Key enhancements include the addition of filters such as `limit`, `tag`, and `private` across various tools, ensuring alignment with backend functionalities. Notably, the `list_apis` tool now efficiently applies server-side filtering, improving performance and accuracy.

items.title: Enhance Group Management with Create and Invite APIs in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new APIs for managing WeChat groups: creating a group and inviting members. The `POST /api/groups` endpoint allows users to initiate a group chat with at least two contacts, while the `POST /api/groups/invite` endpoint enables adding members to an existing group. Both functionalities have been validated through live testing, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance Task Cards with Audio/Video Prompts in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of missing prompts and reference media on Seedance task cards. Key changes include the addition of computed properties for displaying prompts, reference audio, video, and images, ensuring all relevant media is visible. The implementation aligns with existing UI conventions and passes all review checks.
